column15: Best for: Course creators wanting all-in-one. Good balance of features and ease of use.
column16: High transaction fees on free tier (10%), can get expensive, community features basic, limited customization, mobile app limited to iOS (Android users out of luck).
column17: TESTED: Built mini-course with 10 lessons. Setup was straightforward (upload videos, create pricing, publish). Payment processing worked flawlessly. However, community features were basic (just discussion threads under lessons). At 10% transaction fee on free tier, lost significant revenue on $200 course ($20 per sale!). Upgraded to Pro to remove fees but $149/month is steep. Perfect for course creators wanting all-in-one, thinkific is better for value.

column15: Best for: Course creators wanting flexibility. Better value than Teachable with similar features.
column16: Community is paid add-on ($50/mo extra), affiliate program separate add-on, can get expensive with add-ons, limited customization on lower tiers, mobile app is paid add-on.
column17: TESTED: Built full course with 50 lessons. Course builder was more flexible than Teachable (could customize lesson order, prerequisites). Drip scheduling worked perfectly. However, community required separate $50/month add-on (expensive). Affiliate program also separate ($?). At $149/month for Basic, felt expensive compared to Podia ($39/month). Perfect for course creators needing flexibility, Podia is better value.

column14: Best for: Serious course creators wanting everything. Most features but highest price.
column15: Very expensive (starting at $149/mo), steep learning curve (feature-rich), can feel overwhelming for beginners, email marketing basic compared to dedicated tools, pricing tiers restrictive.
column16: TESTED: Built signature course with community. Kajabi is truly all-in-one (course, community, email marketing, website, pipeline builder). Community features were robust (better than Teachable/Thinkific). Course delivery was flawless. However, $149/month starting price is hard to swallow for new creators. Learning curve was steep (so many features). Email marketing was basic compared to ConvertKit. Perfect if budget allows, use Podia for better value.

column13: Best for: Course creators wanting simplicity + community. Best value for price.
column14: Limited course customization, fewer integrations, basic analytics, less mature platform (fewer features than Kajabi), community features not as robust.
column15: TESTED: Built course + community. Setup was dead simple (upload content, set price, launch). Community included in Mover plan ($39/month!) vs Teachful's add-on. Digital downloads included (could sell templates, ebooks). However, course customization was limited (couldn't create complex learning paths). Analytics were basic. Perfect for course creators wanting simplicity and value, Kajabi is better if you need advanced features.

column13: Best for: Community-first creators. Best community features
column15: Course delivery is secondary (community-first), limited course customization, no affiliate program built-in, expensive for what you get, steeper learning curve.
column16: TESTED: Built community-first membership. Community features were incredible (spaces, live events, chat, member profiles). Felt like a private social network. However, course delivery was limited (no quizzes, certificates, advanced drip scheduling). No built-in affiliate program (had to use Thrivecart). At $99/month, felt expensive if community is your main need. Perfect for community-first creators, use Kajabi/Podia for course-first.
